task_completion.description: OpenBCI GUI is open and running in playback mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI interface is visible, and the system control panel indicates it is in 'Playback' mode (reading from a file rather than live streaming).

task_completion.description: The correct EEG recording file is loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filename 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt' is visible in the playback control panel, file selection dialog, or system status area.

task_completion.description: The Filters configuration window or panel is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A window or widget specifically for configuring channel filters (often showing rows for each channel) is open and visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Channel 1 is configured to use a Bandpass filter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Filters window, the row corresponding to Channel 1 (Row 1) has its filter type set to 'Bandpass'.

task_completion.description: Channel 1 filter range is set to 4.0 - 8.0 Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Filters window, the frequency values for Channel 1 (Row 1) are explicitly set to 4.0 and 8.0.

task_completion.description: Channel 2 is configured to use a Bandpass filter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Filters window, the row corresponding to Channel 2 (Row 2) has its filter type set to 'Bandpass'.

task_completion.description: Channel 2 filter range is set to 8.0 - 13.0 Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Filters window, the frequency values for Channel 2 (Row 2) are explicitly set to 8.0 and 13.0.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard OpenBCI GUI interface (with EEG graphs, head plot, and control panels) rather than a text editor modifying a configuration file.

integrity.description: Filter configurations were set via the GUI interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the agent clicking through the GUI menus to open the filters window and inputting the values manually, rather than executing a terminal command to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The final screenshot captures the required visual evidence.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final screenshot shows the OpenBCI GUI with the active filter configurations clearly visible, not just a blank screen, a terminal window, or cropped text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target recording file is '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Recordings/OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt'. The expected screenshot path is '/home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/custom_filters.png'. The VLM must strictly check the Filters window: Row 1 (Channel 1) must be set to a Bandpass filter with values 4.0 and 8.0 Hz. Row 2 (Channel 2) must be set to a Bandpass filter with values 8.0 and 13.0 Hz.
